Title: Genomic basis underlying the metabolome-mediated drought adaptation of maize

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylation/conversion of DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc in the benzoxazinoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with/consumes the target metabolite by converting it to a downstream product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for DIMBOA-Glc accumulation divergence under drought; natural causal variant reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes maize drought tolerance and reduces DIMBOA-Glc accumulation under drought stress.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolite involved in insect and pathogen resistance; in this study higher DIMBOA/DIMBOA-Glc negatively affected drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Diverse inbred lines; B73; CIMBL55; CIMBL70; CIMBL144; Zong3 × teosinte HIF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Bx12 expression is enhanced after drought stress; higher in TE deletion relative to insertion backgrounds consistent with prior report.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under drought stress in maize leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/mQTL mapping; eQTL/expression analysis; natural variant association of CACTA TE insertion/deletion; validation in F2 and HIF linkage populations; metabolite profiling; exogenous DIMBOA application
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural variation at Bx12, including a CACTA TE InDel, is associated with DIMBOA-Glc levels, especially under drought. 2. Drought increases Bx12 expression while lower DIMBOA-Glc is associated with greater drought survival. 3. TE deletion allele confers higher survival in association panel, F2 populations, and HIF lines. 4. Exogenous DIMBOA decreases survival, supporting that Bx12 promotes drought tolerance likely by lowering DIMBOA-Glc through its conversion to HDMBOA-Glc.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics: OMIX419; RNA-seq: CRA004467; GWAS and eQTL using maize association panel genotype resource at maizego.org
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus for natural variation analyses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal variant is a CACTA transposon insertion/deletion in Bx12 previously shown to regulate expression. TE insertion allele appears selected during domestication/spread to US temperate regions, suggesting tradeoff between drought tolerance and other agronomic/defense traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use favorable TE deletion allele or Bx12-enhancing strategies as marker-assisted or editing targets to breed maize with improved drought tolerance; may also couple with lepidopteran resistance due to benzoxazinoid remodeling, but tradeoffs from domestication-selected insertion allele should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible selection balance between drought tolerance and other agronomic traits or defense-related traits at the Bx12 locus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to anthranilate formation in the tryptophan biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is increased in ZmGLK44-overexpressing plants under drought, consistent with promotion of tryptophan accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes maize drought tolerance when applied exogenously.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZmGLK44 transgenic maize; B73 background likely used for promoter cloning
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in ZmGLK44-positive transgenic plants under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under ZmGLK44 induction in drought-stressed plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and RT-qPCR in ZmGLK44 transgenic plants; promoter-LUC assay tested but no activation detected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASA2 is a key tryptophan biosynthetic gene induced in ZmGLK44 overexpression plants under drought. 2. Its induction correlates with elevated indole and tryptophan accumulation. 3. ASA2 promoter fragment tested was not directly activated by ZmGLK44 in protoplast assay.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: CRA004467 for study-wide transcriptomes; transgenic RNA-seq accession not separately stated
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ASA2 promoter fragment (reporter in LUC assay)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly investigated as a key biosynthetic enzyme downstream of core regulator ZmGLK44.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with ZmGLK44 to enhance tryptophan-linked drought tolerance, though direct engineering value was not tested independently.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes phosphoribosyl transfer step in tryptophan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is increased in ZmGLK44-overexpressing plants under drought, consistent with promotion of tryptophan accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes maize drought tolerance when applied exogenously.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in ZmGLK44-positive transgenic plants under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under ZmGLK44 induction in drought-stressed plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and RT-qPCR in ZmGLK44 transgenic plants; promoter-LUC assay tested but no activation detected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAT1 is induced by ZmGLK44 overexpression under drought. 2. Its induction is associated with elevated indole and tryptophan accumulation. 3. PAT1 promoter fragment tested was not directly activated by ZmGLK44 in protoplast assay.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: CRA004467 for study-wide transcriptomes; transgenic RNA-seq accession not separately stated
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAT1 promoter fragment (reporter in LUC assay)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly investigated as a key biosynthetic enzyme downstream of core regulator ZmGLK44.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway-stacking target with ZmGLK44 for drought-adaptive tryptophan enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of indole and serine to L-tryptophan.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Direct terminal enzyme and direct ZmGLK44 target in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by ZmGLK44 and contributes to increased tryptophan accumulation under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes maize drought tolerance when applied exogenously.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZmGLK44 transgenic maize; B73 for promoter cloning/protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in ZmGLK44-positive transgenic plants under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under ZmGLK44 induction in drought-stressed plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and RT-qPCR in transgenic plants; metabolite profiling of indole and tryptophan; promoter-LUC transient assay in maize protoplasts demonstrating activation of TSB2p2 by ZmGLK44
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TSB2 is a key terminal tryptophan biosynthetic gene induced in ZmGLK44-overexpressing plants under drought. 2. ZmGLK44 directly activates a TSB2 promoter fragment in maize protoplasts. 3. Elevated TSB2 expression is accompanied by increased indole and tryptophan and improved drought tolerance in ZmGLK44 transgenic plants.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: CRA004467 for study-wide transcriptomes; transgenic RNA-seq accession not separately stated
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TSB2 promoter fragments TSB2p1 and TSB2p2 used in LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the only tested tryptophan-pathway gene shown to be a direct transcriptional target of ZmGLK44.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for pathway engineering or stacking with ZmGLK44 to boost tryptophan-associated drought tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GARP subfamily MYB transcription factor; GLK family
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TSB2 directly; upregulates ASA2, PAT1, and TSB2 expression under drought.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Environment-specific hub regulator controlling drought-induced amino acid metabolism, especially tryptophan accumulation, and thereby drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Tryptophan biosynthesis and broader drought-responsive amino acid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tryptophan accumulation and enhances maize drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves drought survival when externally applied; likely supports ABA signaling, photosynthesis, chlorophyll maintenance, and water use efficiency under drought.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Transgenic maize lines #1 and #10; B73 and B104 used for exogenous tryptophan assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as a drought-specific hub gene associated/correlated with 30 drought-responsive metabolites.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally analyzed by drought-inducible overexpression under drought conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; drought-inducible overexpression using RD101 promoter; exogenous tryptophan treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene-metabolite network analysis; inducible overexpression in transgenic maize; metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; luciferase transactivation assay in maize protoplasts; drought survival and physiological measurements
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RD101p (drought-inducible) for transgenic overexpression; 35S::ZmGLK44 effector in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGLK44 is a drought-specific hub TF associated with 30 drought-responsive metabolites, many of them amino acid related. 2. Drought-inducible overexpression elevates tryptophan and related metabolites mainly under drought. 3. ZmGLK44 upregulates tryptophan biosynthetic genes ASA2, PAT1, and TSB2, and directly activates TSB2 promoter fragment in protoplasts. 4. Overexpression improves photosynthetic rate, chlorophyll, water use efficiency, lowers ion leakage, and increases survival after drought.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics: OMIX419; RNA-seq: CRA004467; transgenic RNA-seq described in study, accession not separately given
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty under well-watered conditions for gas exchange, WUE, or chlorophyll was reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes additional regulation of ABA-signaling genes (ZmPYL12; ZmSnRK2.1; SnRK2.4; ZmPP2C-A4; ZmPP2C-A7; ZmRD17; ZmRAB18; ZmLEA), suggesting broader drought-response control beyond tryptophan biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering/breeding target for simultaneous improvement of drought tolerance and potentially yield; suitable for drought-inducible expression or allele mining/engineering to enhance Trp-associated adaptive metabolism.
